sense_text: feeling doubt about sth; not sure
[formal] a drug or substance that makes you feel more active and gives you more energy
if a substance or activity is addictive, it makes people unable to stop using it or doing it
having lost too much water from your body
made or done badly and with not enough care
belonging to an early stage in the development of humans or animals
a young animal or plant in the very early stages of development before birth, or before coming out of its egg or seed, especially a human egg in the first eight weeks after fertilization

the state of being extremely tired or ill, either physically or mentally, because you have worked too hard
the state of having a close personal relationship with sb
[formal, law] to confirm a legal decision
if a plant withers or sth withers it, it dries up and dies
the act or process of making a position of power or success stronger so that it is more likely to continue
to cause pain or trouble to sb/sth over a period of time
the act of doing deliberate damage to equipment, transport, machines, etc. to prevent an enemy from using them, or to protest about sth
a short, interesting or funny story about a real person or event
returning to its original shape after being bent, stretched, or pressed
believing that other people dislike you or want to harm you, when there is no reason or evidence for this
trouble or problems, especially in connection with what you need or would like yourself
a feeling of great fear about sth that might or will happen in the future; a thing that causes this feeling
a lack of confidence about yourself or your relationships with other people; sth that makes you feel like this
growing thickly and strongly in a way that is attractive; covered in healthy grass and plants
[disapproving] a strong desire for more wealth, possessions, power, etc. than a person needs
wanting to be successful in life
work, especially physical work
a period of time during the year when the members of a parliament, committee, etc. do not meet

interesting and unusual enough to attract attention
a long narrow piece of paper, metal, cloth, etc.
to take off all or most of your clothes or another person’s clothes
logical and well organized; easy to understand and clear
able to be used together
to obey a rule, law, etc.
to say that sth that sb else has said is wrong, and that the opposite is true
[formal] to arrange for people to come together for a formal meeting
[formal] to press or push sth down, especially part of a machine
to recognize that there is a difference between people or things; to show a difference between people or things
to gradually destroy the surface of sth through the action of wind, rain, etc.; to be gradually destroyed in this way
suggested without being directly expressed
[informal] a vehicle with a system of gears operated by the driver using a gear lever
to try to end a situation between two or more people or groups who disagree by talking to them and trying to find things that everyone can agree on
to move from one town, country, etc. to go and live and/or work in another
to make a substance pure by taking other substances out of it
stiff and difficult to move or bend
a contagious disease spreads by close contact between people
based on your own ideas or opinions rather than facts and therefore sometimes unfair
behaving in a way that is not sensible and is typical of people who are much younger

[biology] a process in which the genetic material of a person, a plant or an animal changes in structure when it is passed on to children, etc., causing different physical characteristics to develop; a change of this kind
a feeling of being extremely tired, usually because of hard work or exercise
to make a wild animal used to living with or working for humans
wrong information created by artificial intelligence
a statement containing two opposite ideas that make it seem impossible or unlikely, although it is probably true; the use of this in writing
pleased to welcome guests; generous and friendly to visitors
to eat all of sth quickly, especially because you are very hungry
[formal] to make sth legally recognized
the quality of having the same size and shape
having a calm and confident manner and in control of your feelings and behaviour

the act of deliberately making sb believe sth that is not true (= of deceiving them)
a permanent physical or mental condition that makes it difficult to do some things that most other people can do
able to think quickly and in an intelligent way
an area where wild birds or animals are protected and encouraged to produce young
a holy building or the part of it that is considered the most holy
[medical] connected with treatment that repairs the body or a part of it
a small waterfall, especially one of several falling down a steep slope with rocks
a coloured powder that is mixed with a liquid to produce paint, etc.

the period of time or the process of giving birth to a baby
to plan and direct the course of ship, plane, car etc., for example by using a map

a strong board that you jump on and use to help you jump high in diving and gymnastics
a list of all sb’s social media activity on a particular website or software program in the order in which it happened, with the most recent activity shown at the beginning of the list

the way that sth, especially a piece of clothing, fits; the way that sb/sth fits into sth
if your eyes pop or pop out, they suddenly open fully because you are surprised or excited
the uniform that is worn by the members of a sports team when they are playing
[informal] to damage a car very badly, so that it is not worth repairing it
to move from one part of the world to another according to the season
to speak to sb about a problem or difficult situation
to describe a process or the development of sth
to make sb feel very upset or anxious by reminding them of a trauma or bad experience
to fasten or hang sth on sth else using a hook; to be fastened or hanging in this way
[formal] a boat or ship
[formal, informal] a sum of money that is paid into a bank account
(in the British political system) the amount of money that a candidate in an election to Parliament has to pay, and that is returned if he/she gets enough votes
difficult and not certain to continue or to be successful
